breaking_news.title: Trivy Vulnerability Scanner Compromised in Supply Chain Attack Targeting CI/CD Secrets
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors known as TeamPCP compromised the widely used Trivy vulnerability scanner, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases and GitHub Actions. This supply chain breach impacts thousands of CI/CD workflows that rely on the tool for security scanning, potentially exposing sensitive pipeline secrets.

The attack could trigger a cascade of additional compromises if impacted organizations do not immediately rotate their secrets and treat all pipeline credentials as breached. Security firms traced the root cause to an incomplete credential rotation after a previous breach, allowing attackers to return and introduce malicious commits.

breaking_news.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco Firewall Zero-Day Weeks Before Patch Release
breaking_news.summary:
The Interlock ransomware group exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software weeks before a patch was publicly released. This flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20131, allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary Java code as root on affected devices.

CISA has added this vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering federal agencies to patch by Sunday. The discovery highlights the urgency of the threat, as attackers have been actively weaponizing this deserialization flaw since late January to compromise network security infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword Exploit Kit Uses Zero-Days for Full iOS Device Takeover
breaking_news.summary:
A new iOS exploit kit named DarkSword has been identified using six vulnerabilities, including three zero-days, to achieve full device compromise. Multiple threat actors, including surveillance vendors and suspected nation-state groups, have utilized this toolkit in campaigns targeting Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Ukraine.

The exploit chain relies on memory corruption flaws in JavaScriptCore and the iOS kernel to steal sensitive data from unpatched iPhones. Apple has urged users to update their devices immediately, as simply visiting a malicious website can trigger the infection chain on vulnerable operating system versions.

breaking_news.title: Oracle Patches Critical Unauthenticated RCE Flaw in Identity Manager
breaking_news.summary:
Oracle released an emergency security update to address a critical remote code execution vulnerability in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-21992, carries a maximum CVSS score of 9.8 and allows unauthenticated attackers to take control of systems over HTTP.

Successful exploitation could result in full system compromise, risking severe impact on data confidentiality and availability for enterprise identity management infrastructure. Oracle strongly recommends that customers apply the provided updates or mitigations as soon as possible to prevent unauthorized access.

breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked Handala Group Wipes Stryker Systems via Microsoft Intune Abuse
breaking_news.summary:
The pro-Iranian threat actor Handala conducted a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker, wiping approximately 80,000 devices. Instead of using traditional malware, the attackers compromised a legitimate Microsoft Intune endpoint management system to execute the wipe command.

CISA and the FBI have issued warnings urging organizations to harden their Microsoft Intune configurations following this incident. The attack demonstrates a sophisticated method of abusing trusted enterprise management tools to bypass security controls and cause operational disruption.

breaking_news.title: WorldLeaks Ransomware Group Breaches Los Angeles Metro System
breaking_news.summary:
The WorldLeaks ransomware group hit the Los Angeles Metro system, forcing a shutdown of internal administrative systems and disrupting station arrival displays. Unauthorized activity on internal networks prompted the transit agency to limit access, causing significant operational issues for riders.

While rail and bus services continue to run normally, the attack highlights the vulnerability of critical public infrastructure to ransomware threats. Two Bay Area cities also declared emergencies following separate ransomware attacks, indicating a broader campaign against municipal services.

breaking_news.title: PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE on 7,500+ Magento Sites
breaking_news.summary:
A critical vulnerability dubbed PolyShell in the Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API allows attackers to upload executable files without authentication. Since late February, hackers have defaced over 7,500 sites across 15,000 hostnames in a global hacking campaign targeting e-commerce platforms.

The flaw enables remote code execution and account takeover on versions up to 2.4.9-alpha2, exposing many online stores to compromise. Adobe has fixed the issue in a pre-release version, but many stores remain vulnerable due to web server configurations that enable RCE or stored XSS.

breaking_news.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Targeting Signal and WhatsApp Users
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI and CISA warned that Russian intelligence-linked threat actors are conducting phishing campaigns to compromise commercial messaging applications like Signal and WhatsApp. The goal is to seize control of accounts belonging to individuals with high intelligence value through targeted social engineering.

This campaign represents a significant threat to secure communications, as attackers aim to bypass encryption by compromising the endpoints directly. Users of these encrypted messaging apps are urged to remain vigilant against suspicious links and verification requests that could lead to account takeover.
